description: Physical markers database schema for clinical tracking software — baseline vs follow-up progression model. v1.1 adds rendering hints (direction_of_good, default_chart, comparison_mode, client_portal_visibility, client_view_chart) per metric. These are factory defaults; per-EP overrides live in the practice_test_settings table.

result_record.description: Shape of a single test result entry stored in the database

unit.description: Unit as defined in schema metric

session_id.description: Groups all metrics recorded in one session

is_baseline.description: True for the first recorded session — all subsequent sessions compared against this

direction_of_good.higher: Larger value is a better outcome
direction_of_good.lower: Smaller value is a better outcome
direction_of_good.target_range: Clinically meaningful range exists; deviations either side are flagged
direction_of_good.context_dependent: No general direction; chart engine renders neutral until per-client context is set

default_chart.bar: Categorical comparison at one timepoint (e.g. KOOS subscales)
default_chart.radar: Multi-axis snapshot — reserved for ≥4 related metrics
default_chart.asymmetry_bar: Left vs right with LSI annotation — default for bilateral metrics
default_chart.target_zone: Value plotted within a clinical reference band

comparison_mode.vs_baseline: Each session compared to first recorded session

client_portal_visibility.auto: Result appears in client Reports tab as soon as logged
client_portal_visibility.on_publish: Held until clinician publishes with optional framing text
client_portal_visibility.never: Never visible to client (clinician-only screening tools)

client_view_chart.line: Line chart over time (used sparingly client-side)
client_view_chart.milestone: Baseline → Latest with delta — strips noisy middle data
client_view_chart.narrative_only: No chart; clinician summary text only
